The instructor will bisque fire, glaze, and glaze fire the projects. Students can expect to use a variety of clay hand building techniques.

One lb of clay per person.

All Ages welcome

payload.title: North Woods Art Tour
payload.description:
Across Vilas, Oneida, Iron, and Lincoln counties, North Woods artists open their studios and galleries for a behind-the-scenes look at how the work gets made. Meet painters, potters, photographers, furniture makers, wood carvers, jewelers, and artists working in glass, fiber, and mixed media — right where they create.

The tour is free and self-guided. Browse the map and artist pages at https://www.northwoodsarttour.com to plan a route around the studios and processes that interest you most. Brochures are also available at area chambers of commerce, galleries, and local businesses.

General tour information: Jack Stayer, 715.588.7447

payload.title: FREE Kids Fishing Tip Programs-by Headwaters Chapter  Muskies, Inc.
payload.description:
Free fishing seminars for children ages 5 to 13 will be held at the Vilas County Fairgrounds in the shelter house , Eagle River, WI

Topics covered will include fish identification, size limits, license requirements, rigging slip-bobbers, tying knots, fish habitat, how to net fish, how to handle fish, catch-and-release, boating safety, being responsible to others and the environment, casting techniques, and preventing the spread of aquatic invasive species.

payload.title: National Championship Musky Open
payload.description:
2026 Musky Open –  August 20 – August 23

The Eagle River Area Chamber of Commerce & Visitor Center and Musky Clubs Alliance of Wisconsin, Inc. are pleased to announce the 40th Annual National Championship Musky Open Tournament

Our tournament headquarters will be at Vilas County Fairgrounds on Highway 70 West, just blocks from downtown Eagle River. Registration and orientation will be at the headquarters where you will receive your registration packet.

Please note the registration hours, briefing times and fishing hours. All contestants must register in person, receive all tournament information, and attend a briefing before fishing by Saturday morning at 6:30 am.

We will mail you a confirmation registration card. Please keep your copy and bring it to tournament registration.

It’s going to be a great tournament! Remember, a portion of the proceeds always goes for fish stocking and habitat improvement. If you have any questions regarding the tournament, please call the Eagle River Area Chamber of Commerce & Visitors Center at 800-359-6315.

Note: Food is available for purchase at Tournament Headquarters Thursday and Saturday night from 5pm – 9pm and on Sunday from 11am – 3pm.  Food is provided by the Greater Eagle River Tennis Association.

Please be sure your boat is clean and free of Aquatic Invasive Species prior to leaving and entering another lake.

Registration

The entry fee will be $75.00 on or before August 1, 2026.  Please note that all walk-in entry fees at the tournament headquarters will be $80.00 cash. We would like as many entrants as possible to pre-register before August 1, 2026. This makes it easier for the tournament to have available the proper number of hats, t-shirts and any other information. Should you need to register after August 1, 2026 you will need to do so at the tournament headquarters.  No pre-registration will be accepted after August 1, 2026.

Please note the registration hours, briefing times and fishing hours. All contestants must register in person, receive all tournament information, and attend a briefing before fishing by Saturday morning at 6:30 am.

To be eligible for the Early Bird Drawing, you must register before May 15, 2026. Our Early Bird Prize is two nights lodging in the Eagle River Area (subject to availability).

  1. REGISTER ONLINE – CLICK HERE
    Please note that credit card transactions are subject to a $4.00 transaction fee.
  2. REGISTER BY MAIL FORM  – CLICK HERE

    Print, Complete, and Mail this Registration Form to: 

    Eagle River Chamber of Commerce & Visitors Center
    PO Box 1917-MT
    Eagle River, WI 54521

  3. REGISTER IN PERSON

    Drop off your Registration Form at the Eagle River Chamber of Commerce and Visitors Center prior to August 1, 2026.

    Or register at Tournament Headquarters  (Vilas County Fairgrounds) on Thursday evening, August 20, 2026 – Saturday morning, August 22, 2026

Schedule

Printable Schedule – CLICK HERE

Thursday, August 20, 2026

Registration – 5:00 pm to 9:00 pm

Briefing – 6:30 pm, 7:30 pm and 9:00 pm

Seminars – 6:00 pm

Food – 5:00 pm to 9:00 pm

Friday, August 21, 2026

Registration

6:00 am to 6:30 am (morning)

5:00 pm to 7:30 pm (evening)

Briefing

6:30 am (morning)

7:30 pm (evening)

Fishing Hours (optional)

6:00 am to 5:00 pm

(Fish Verification Slips can be turned in for fish caught and released on Friday at tournament headquarters between 5 pm and 8 pm.)

Saturday, August 22, 2026

Registration – 6:00 am to 6:30 am

Briefing – 6:30 am

Fishing Hours (optional) – 6:00 am to 5:00 pm

(Fish Verification Slips for fish caught and released on Friday and/or Saturday must be turned in on Saturday evening at tournament headquarters between 6 pm – 8 pm.  Friday and Saturday fish not turned in on Saturday evening will not be scored.)

Door Prizes – 6:00 pm

You must be present to win.

Food – 5:00 pm – 9:00 pm

Sunday, August 23, 2026

Fishing Hours – 6:00 am to 11:00 am

(Fish Verification Slips for fish caught and released on Sunday must be turned in at tournament headquarters by 12:30 pm.  Fish turned in after 12:30 pm will not be scored.)

Tournament Results, Door Prizes & Grand Drawing – 1:30 pm

You must be present to win.

Food – 11:00 am – 3:00 pm

 

Lake Families

There are seven (7) “Lake Families” available to fish. These families can be requested and will be assigned on a first-come-first-serve basis. Each lake family is limited to 500 participants.  Once a lake family is filled you will have to fish your 2nd choice. You must fish your assigned lake family at all times during the tournament.  No changes or exceptions.

Lake Family 1: The Eagle River Chain Area – 10 lakes of the Eagle Chain from dam to dam, Lower Ninemile Lake, Sevenmile Lake, Kentuck Lake, and Deerskin Lake.

Lake Family 2: The Three Lakes Chain – All lakes in the Three Lakes Chain from dam up river.

Lake Family 3: The “Big Lakes” Family – North Twin Lake, South Twin Lake, Long Lake, Big Sand Lake, Lac Vieux Desert Lake and Pioneer Lake

Lake Family 4: The “Western” Family – Big St. Germain Lake, Little St. Germain Lake, Star Lake, Found Lake, Lost Lake, Laura Lake, Little John, Big Muskellunge Lake, Irving Lake, Ballard Lake, White Birch Lake, Plum Lake and Razorback Lake

Lake Family 5: The Sugar Camp Chain Area – Five Lakes of the Sugar Camp Chain, Gilmore Lake, Pickerel Lake (off Hwy. J), Rainbow Flowage, Little Arbor Vitae Lake and Big Arbor Vitae Lake

Lake Family 6: The “Eastern” Family – Pickerel Lake (off Hwy. G), Upper and Lower Buckatabon Lake, Muskellunge Lake (off Hwy. G), Snipe Lake, Boot Lake and Stella Lake

Lake Family 7: The “Northern Lakes” Family – Fishtrap Lake, Palmer Lake, White Sand Lake, High Lake, Tenderfoot Lake, Boulder Lake, Wildcat Lake, Little Crooked Lake and Trout Lake.

Divisions

Trophies are awarded for the winners of each division. Winners are determined by a point basis by length in inches. Anglers may be eligible for more than one division.

Most points accumulated
Trophies awarded for 1st, 2nd, 3rd, 4th, 5th, and 6th places.

Largest musky registered for each lake family.
The largest registered musky measured in inches in each lake family will receive a trophy.

Husband/Wife Trophy
A registered husband and wife pair can enter to win this category by catching the most fish in inches.

Youth Trophy
A youth (8 – 15 years) can enter to win this category by catching the most fish in inches.

Largest Fish
The largest registered fished during the tournament will receive a graphite replica mount from Fittante Taxidermy/Replicas.

Rules and Regulations

2026 Printable  Updated Rules and Regulations  – CLICK HERE

Eligibility & Registration

  1. All persons are eligible to enter the National Championship Musky Open on August 21, 22 & 23 2026. All persons under 18 years of age must be accompanied by a parent or guardian.  Due to safety issues, children under the age of 8 at the time of the tournament are not eligible for this tournament.
  2. Entries for pre-registration must be postmarked no later than August 1, 2026 in order to be received as a pre-registration. Contestants whose entries are received prior to May 15, 2026 will be eligible for an Early Bird Drawing – a two night stay at an area lodging facility in Eagle River, WI.  All pre-registration entry fees are $75 per person.
  3. No entries will be accepted after an August 1, 2026 postmark. After August 1, 2026, entries need to be done at on-site registration at the tournament headquarters-Vilas County Fairgrounds.  All on-site entry fees are $80 per person and must be paid in cash.
  4. Cancellation. No refund can be given should you need to cancel your entry into the tournament after August 1, 2026. Credit for entry can be transferred to the 2027 tournament.
  5. Registration is available on-line at www.muskyopen.com

Requirements

  1. Each boat and contestant will be required to abide by all of the State of Wisconsin’s boating and fishing laws.
  2. Each entrant must personally sign their own attached insurance waiver form along with the registration form.  A parent/guardian must sign for a minor. No exceptions
  3. All partners must be listed on the registration form
  4. All contestants must register in person at the tournament headquarters and must attend one mandatory tournament rules briefing before fishing.  Report your attendance of a briefing to your lake family.

Boats & Equipment

  1. All boats must meet U.S. Coast Guard and State of Wisconsin boating rules and safety standards and must be registered in accordance with regulations of the home state of the contestant owning the boat.
  2. All boats must be clean and free of Aquatic Invasive Species prior to leaving and entering another lake.
  3. Cameras/digital cameras are recommended.
  4. Forward Facing sonar-live sonar and all other real-time imaging-like technology, will not be allowed to be used during the tournament and will be banned from use 2 hours before the start of the 1st day of the tournament, until the end of the last day of the tournament. these units do not have to be removed from the boat, just the “live” feature on unit cannot be used during the tournament. other screens such as regular sonar, non-live version of side and down imaging, GPS mapping and such can still be used on these units. We believe this is a family-fun tournament and want to keep it fair to all.

Fishing

  1. Registered participants who immediately release muskies which are 34″ and over will be counted and scored on ALL tournament lakes. Measure to the nearest 1/4″.
  2. No fishing from bridges, piers, docks or the shore.
  3. Only muskies caught legally on a hook and line will qualify for scoring.
  4. Each person fishing is allowed only one (1) line in the water at any time. No live bait allowed.
  5. Row trolling is permitted. No motor trolling allowed.
  6. There shall be no less than two (2) registered contestants per boat at all times while fishing and all people in the boat during fishing hours must be registered tournament anglers.
  7. Tournament hats MUST be worn while fishing.
  8. Only muskies caught and released on Friday, Saturday or Sunday during tournament hours will be accepted for scoring.  Verification catch slips will be issued to each contestant.
  9. All muskies caught must be verified and released immediately. Verification catch slips may be done by fellow contestants from a different boat or the nearest person not in your boat.  All slips must be turned in to Tournament Headquarters as soon as possible.  Fish caught and released on Friday or Saturday MUST be registered by 8pm on Saturday night and fish caught and released on Sunday must be registered by 12:30pm on Sunday.  Failure to do so will result in the fish being disqualified from scoring.
  10. This is a total catch & release tournament. Contestants may resume fishing upon successfully verifying and releasing their musky.  If a musky is killed, the contestant must cease fishing for that day. As the legal bag limit is one (1) fish per day.
  11. In case of a tie, the winner will be determined by earliest fish registration time on verification slip. Any disputes will be settled by the Official Tournament Committee and their decision is final and binding.

Alcoholic Beverages

NO alcoholic beverages may be consumed while fishing by contestants or tournament officials.  Please note due to licensing, no carry-ins are allowed at the Vilas County Fairgrounds. 

Drawings

Bring a “New Angler” Form

Any prior angler of the National Championship Musky Open who gets a NEW Angler to enter the 2026 tournament, can enter their name into a special drawing for a musky rod.  The more people you have entered, the more chances you have to win this great musky rod.  You do not have to fish with this person(s), just recommend our great event and have the new angler register to be in the tournament. 

HELP FIGHT HUNGER

Get a chance to win a musky reel and several other musky and non-musky related prizes by donating a non-perishable food item or cash.  For each food item you donate, you will receive one chance.  For each dollar you donate, you will receive two chances in the Help Fight Hunger drawing.  Non-perishable food items and cash donations can be donated on Thursday, Friday, and Saturday evenings at the Help Fight Hunger Tent located at tournament headquarters. (Food donations will not be accepted with expired expiration dates.)  All food donations will be donated to the Vilas County Food Pantry and must be received by 7:00pm on Saturday evening.  Cash donations are encouraged as this allows the Vilas County Food Pantry to purchase specifically needed items for their shelves.  

Door Prizes and Raffle Prizes

The Grand Door Prize: 2026 Model 895 DC Recon Boat with 115 Mercury Outboard Motor and Trailer

Other prizes have been supplied by Fittante Taxidermy/Replicas, Diawa, Mercury, H3O Polarized, St. Croix Rods, Enticer Tackle, Suick Lure Company, Eagle River Inn and Z Leaders Fishing Rigs.

Early Bird

To be eligible for the Early Bird Drawing, you must register before May 15, 2026. Our Early Bird Prize is two nights lodging in the Eagle River Area. (subject to availability).

payload.title: 46th Eagle River Car and Truck Show
payload.description:
Featuring Classic Cars and Trucks

Enjoy beautiful classic cars and trucks on display, as well as tasty food, beverages, music and lots of fun. Vote for your favorite vehicle in the People’s Choice Award.

 

Do you own a classic vehicle and would like to show it off?  Come display your classic car or truck for a chance to take home a trophy. Trophies will be awarded in various categories – see categories below.

Classic Car and Truck Show Vehicle Registration 

Registration to showcase your classic vehicle can be done prior to the event, which is suggested, but we will also take registrations on the day of the event.  Registration per vehicle is $20.  The show is limited to 225 entries.

Register Online – Click Here

Printable version of the Registration Form – Click Here

Click here for a printable version of the Event Map

Day of Event Information:

Registration Time:  Begin registration and parking of vehicles at 7am and until 10am.  (Enter Registration Area at the corner of Division and First Street.)  Staff will check you in and direct you to your parking spot.  The Show is open to the public from 9am-3pm.  Judging will begin at 11am.  Vehicles who have not registered by 10am can still register and be a part of the show, but will not be judged.  The Awards Ceremony will take place at 2:30pm at the corner of Wall and First Street.

Weather Policy:  This event will take place rain or shine.  Due to unforeseen circumstances and unfavorable weather conditions, the Eagle River Chamber of Commerce reserves the right to cancel this event at any time.  We will do our best to notify participants as early as possible.  Refunds will not be given due to the weather or cancellation of this event.

Vehicle Classes:  Category to be judged in: (Please choose only 1 category letter per vehicle)

  1. Pre-1950
  2. 50’s
  3. 60’s Original
  4. 60’s Modified
  5. 70’S Original
  6. 70’S Modified
  7. 80’S
  8. 90’s
  9. 2000 – 2012
  10. 2013 to Present
  11. Custom Hot Rod
  12. Truck Original
  13. Truck Modified

All Ages welcome

payload.title: Open Mic Lab @ Inspiration Bar, Warehouse Community Arts Center
payload.description:
Open Mic Lab is designed as an evening of artistic sharing. It is so much more than just music; it is dedicated to the experimental performance of all things creative including, but not limited to: comedy, film, music, poetry, storytelling, and theater. It’s a great place to try out a new song or any creative pursuits that need an audience. Performing is not mandatory, just audience members are welcomed. To reserve a performance spot, please register on the Warehouse website.

Open Mic Lab is the first Friday of the month at the Warehouse Community Arts Center

No tickets or reservations needed to attend or preform at this family friendly FREE event.

Stop in and enjoy live, authentic performances in a friendly atmosphere while you wet your whistle!
